D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

The Thin-Lens Equation

slide an object past a lens and trace the three rays that locate its image

A converging lens images an object using three predictable rays. Their crossing point is the image; where it lands depends only on the object distance and the focal length.

Move the object and change the focal length. Inside the focal point the image turns virtual and upright; beyond it, real and inverted. The gold readout gives image distance and magnification.
